title: Environmental Design

content.statement: Your environment is stronger than discipline.
content.question: Which space is fighting you instead of serving you?
sop: Walk through your main workspace and home space and note every visible cue.
Rate the Environment 1-10 for how well it's designed for the behavior you want (see calculation, page 4)
Remove or hide every trigger for the behavior you don't want.
Add one visible cue (object, note, layout change) for the behavior you do want.
Re-audit monthly, environments quietly revert if you don't maintain them.
questions: Which space is fighting you instead of serving you?
What's within arm's reach right now that's pulling you toward a behavior you don't actually want?
If you removed every trigger for your worst habit from your space today, what would actually be left to fight?
content.equation_example: Rate Environment 1-10 (how well it's designed for the behavior you want) and Exposure Time in hours/day spent in it. Environment 8 times Exposure Time 6 equals 48. Higher means your space is quietly pulling you toward the behavior you want, hour after hour. Lower means you're relying on willpower to fight a space that's working against you.
content.closing_statement: Discipline fades. A well-built environment doesn't.

caption:
Principle Nº 135: Environmental Design

Your environment is stronger than discipline.

Ask yourself: Which space is fighting you instead of serving you?
